response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and systems developer with strong web (TypeScript/Node.js) and DLang experience, but the role requires deep, current C++ expertise in industrial automation. Their C++ exposure is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project, with no evidence of IEC 62443/SDL practice, MQTT/industrial protocols, message-broker architectures at scale in C++, or the mentoring-in-security aspect the role demands. The low-latency DLang work at Dunnhumby shows relevant systems-programming instincts, but overall the profile diverges too far from the core requirements.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and knowledge-sharing/workshops
Broad polyglot background spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~7 months, a decade ago) versus a C++-centric role
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), or IEC 62443/SDL security background
Recent career trajectory is web/product and AI tooling, not high-performance native or embedded platform development
